post.description: Set the threshold for triggering capacity usage alerts.

parameters.name: capacityAlertHighThresholdPercent
parameters.description: Threshold of the non-spare capacity of the System that will trigger a high-priority alert, expressed as a percentage. This value must be lower than the capacityAlertCriticalThresholdPercent value.

parameters.name: capacityAlertCriticalThresholdPercent
parameters.description: Threshold of the non-spare capacity of the System that will trigger a critical-priority alert, expressed as a percentage.

parameters.name: allStoragePools
parameters.description: allStoragePools indicates whether the capacity alert trigger should be set for all Storage Pools in the system. systemDefault indicates whether the capacity alert threshold should be used as a default for all newly created Storage Pools.

parameters.name: systemDefault
parameters.description: At least one of these two parameters must have a value of TRUE.

post.description: Create an MDM cluster.

parameters.name: mdmIp
parameters.description: IP address of the MDM that will run the request.

parameters.name: ips
parameters.description: List of IP addresses of the created MDM.

parameters.name: managementIps
parameters.description: List of management IP addresses of the created MDM.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Name of the created MDM. Each MDM name must be unique. Ensure that each server on which the MDM is installed has a unique hostname.
NOTE:	If the error message SERVER_CERTIFICATE_NOT_TRUSTED is returned, the MDM's host name is not a unique name. To solve the problem, change the MDM host's name.

parameters.name: virtIpIntfs
parameters.description: NIC interface(s) to be used by the Primary MDM for virtual IP addresses.

parameters.name: virtIPs
parameters.description: Virtual IP addresses that will be used by the MDM cluster.

parameters.name: updateConfiguration
parameters.description: If the value is set to TRUE and the mdmAddresses parameter in the REST gateway configuration is empty, the mdmAddresses will be populated with the values in ips.

post.description:
Add a standby MDM to the system.

Note: The addStandbyMdm request does not add the IP addresses to the mdmAddresses field. Instead, post an /api/updateConfiguration request to manually update the IP addresses.

parameters.name: ips
parameters.description: List of IP addresses assigned to the new MDM. Can contain both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

parameters.name: managementIps
parameters.description: List of IP addresses used to manage the MDM. Can contain both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

parameters.name: virtIpIntfs
parameters.description: List of NIC interfaces that will be used for virtual IP address.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Port of the new MDM. Default: 9011

parameters.name: forceClean
parameters.description: Clean a previous SDS configuration: TRUE or FALSE

parameters.name: allowAsymmetricIps
parameters.description: Allow the added node to have a different number of IPs from the primary node: TRUE or FALSE

schema.description: ID of the new MDM

parameters.description: List of IP address to be used to manage the MDM.

parameters.name: allowDuplicate
parameters.description: Allow duplicate management IP addresses. If the value is set to TRUE, the command will not fail if there are duplicate IP addresses in other MDMs.

post.description: Change MDM cluster ownership from the current primary MDM to a different MDM.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: MDM ID. Must be an MDM with manager role.

post.description:
Switch the MDM cluster from one operating mode or configuration to another.

If the cluster is expanded, the secondary and tieBreaker nodes will be added according to the expanded size.

If the cluster size is reduced, the secondary and tieBreaker nodes will be removed.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: New cluster mode: OneNode, ThreeNodes, or FiveNodes

parameters.name: addSlaveMdmIdList
parameters.description: List of secondary MDM IDs that will be part of the cluster. A maximum of two IDs are allowed.

parameters.name: addTBIdList
parameters.description: List of secondary MDM IDs to remove from the cluster.

parameters.name: removeSlaveMdmIdList
parameters.description: List of TieBreaker IDs to remove from the cluster.

parameters.name: removeTBIdList
parameters.description: List of TieBreaker IDs to remove from cluster.

parameters.name: allowLeaveFailed
parameters.description: Allow leaving currently failed MDMs in the cluster: TRUE or FALSE

post.description: Replace members of the MDM cluster. Up to half (rounded down) of the cluster members can be replaced at one time.

parameters.name: addSlaveMdmIdList
parameters.description: A list of slave MDM IDs that will be part of the cluster. A maximum of two IDs are allowed.

parameters.name: removeSlaveMdmIdList
parameters.description: A list of slave MDM IDs that will be removed from the cluster. A maximum of two IDs are allowed.

parameters.name: addTBIdList
parameters.description: A list of Tie-Breaker IDs that will be part of the cluster. A maximum of two IDs are allowed.

parameters.name: removeTBIdList
parameters.description: A list of Tie-Breaker IDs that will be removed from the cluster. A maximum of two IDs are allowed.

parameters.name: allowLeaveFailed
parameters.description: Allow leaving currently failed MDMs in the cluster: TRUE or FALSE

The CSR file is used to obtain security certificates from a Certificate Authority and is saved in the specified MDM’s directory:

post.description: Replaces the MDM's security certificate and key. The replacement certificate can be either a self-generated and self-signed certificate, or a certificate generated and signed by an external Certificate Authority.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: MDM ID. Running the command on the primary MDM causes the MDM to restart. It may also cause a brief, single point of failure period due to an MDM switch over. For more information on externally signed security certificates, see the PowerFlex documentation.

post.description: Refresh the exposed cluster node capabilities. This command is usually used to instruct the cluster nodes to check whether LDAP and SSL libraries are installed.
Enable or disable authentication between system components.
Remove all snapshots that belong to a specific consistency group.

parameters.name: snapGroupId
parameters.description: ID of the consistency group. The consistency group ID is displayed when you create the consistency group. To find this ID at a later time, use the /api/types/Volume/instances resource.

parameters.name: volumeClass
parameters.description: Enter the matching volume class to execute this command if volume is managed externally.

numberOfVolumes.description: The number of volumes that were removed because of this operation.

post.description: Creates a snapshot of one or more volumes. When you specify more than one volume, a consistency group is generated and the ID associated with it is returned. The snapshots under the consistency group are taken simultaneously for all listed volumes, thus ensuring their consistency.

parameters.name: snapshotDefs
parameters.description: List of combinations of volumeId (volume ID) and optional snapshotName (name for the snapshot).

parameters.name: volumeClass
parameters.description: Enter the matching volume class to execute this command if volume is managed externally

parameters.name: accessModeLimit
parameters.description: Define whether mapping of the created snapshot volume will have read and write access or limited to read-only access: ReadWrite or ReadOnly

parameters.name: retentionPeriodInMin
parameters.description: Use this option to create secure snapshots that cannot be edited or removed during the specified time period. Specify the retention time period in minutes.

post.description:
Retrieve the delta for the system.

Returns changes of each object type specified in deltaDefs since the last call of this query.

To begin querying deltas, use sessionTag=0 and lastVersion=0. For subsequent queries, use values returned by the previous query.

parameters.name: deltaDefs
parameters.description:
List of combinations of type and lastVersion. Options for type: System, ProtectionDomain, Sds, StoragePool, Device, Volume, VTree, Sdc, Faultset, AccelerationPool, SpSds, SnapshotPolicy
When lastVersion is "0”, all the data is retrieved for the correlated type.

post.description: Reset the SDS oscillating failure counters to zero. This command is useful when you have fixed a problem and want to ensure that an alert is no longer active in the system.

parameters.description: Select one of the following SDS counters to set to zero: MdmSdsNetworkDisconnections, SdsSdsNetworkDisconnections, SdsDecoupled, SdsConfigurationFailures, or SdsReceiveBufferAllocationFailures

post.description: Apply performance parameters to all replication peer systems.

Modify oscillating counter parameters for all SDSs in the system. Use this command to configure the time interval associated with each window type and the number of failures allowed before reporting commences for each window type, per counter.

parameters.name: failureCounter
parameters.description: Select one of the following SDS counters to set to zero: MdmSdsNetworkDisconnections, SdsSdsNetworkDisconnections, SdsDecoupled, SdsConfigurationFailures, or SdsReceiveBufferAllocationFailures

parameters.name: windowType
parameters.description: Window type to be modified: Short, Medium, or Long

parameters.description: New window time interval in seconds. The valid interval range is 1-86400. If the interval is set to 0, the window type is disabled.

parameters.name: failuresThreshold
parameters.description: Maximum number of failures per window type to be ignored before error reporting begins.

parameters.description: List of: devicePath, storagePoolId, deviceName (optional), and mediaType (optional). Possible values for mediaType: HDD or SSD

parameters.description: List of: accelerationDevicePath, accpId, and accelerationDeviceName

parameters.name: forceDeviceTakeover
parameters.description:
Force adding the device.
WARNING:	Use this flag with caution, because all data on the device will be destroyed.
During execution of the command, PowerFlex checks that the device is clear before adding it. If the device is not clear, an error message is returned, and the command fails for that device. If you would like to overwrite existing data on the device by forcing the command, use the forceDeviceTakeover flag.

parameters.name: deviceTestTimeSecs
parameters.description: The maximal test run-time in seconds, or 128 MB data.

parameters.name: deviceTestMode
parameters.description:
One of:
testOnly - Devices will be tested but not used. Later issue activateDevice to start using their capacity.
noTest - Devices will be activated and their capacity will be used by the system without any device testing.
testAndActivate - Devices will be tested, and then their capacity will be used by the system.

If deviceTestMode is not part of the request body, read and write tests will be run on devices before their capacity is used.

Modify the spare capacity reservation policy.

To ensure data protection during server failures, PowerFlex reserves 10 percent of its capacity by default, instead of allowing this capacity to be used for volume allocation. To ensure full system protection in the event of a node failure, the spare capacity must be at least equal to the amount of capacity in the node containing the maximum capacity or the maximum Fault Set capacity.

If all nodes contain equal capacity, you should set the capacity value to at least 1/N of the total capacity (where N is the number of SDS nodes).

post.summary: Add a source volume to a snapshot policy
post.description:
Add a source volume to the snapshot policy specified by ID or name.

You can add multiple source volumes to a snapshot policy, but a specific volume can only be the source volume of a single policy. Only one volume per V-Tree may be used as a source volume of a policy. For more unformation on V-Trees, see "Snapshots" in the Getting to Know PowerFlex Guide.

Remove a source volume from the specified snapshot policy.

When removing the source volume from the policy, you must choose how to handle the snapshots created by the policy (auto snapshots). Your selection depends on if there are locked auto snapshots.

● If the source volume has no auto snapshots, you don't need to use Detach or Remove options.
  
● If the source volume has auto snapshots but none of them are locked, you can choose to detach all snapshots with the Detach option. They become regular snapshots as if they were created manually. If you use the Remove option, the snapshots are deleted.
● If the source volume has locked auto snapshots, you can use the Detach option to turn the auto snapshots into regular snapshots, as if they were created manually. If you use the Remove option, the auto snapshots that are not locked are removed, while the auto snapshots that are locked are detached.

rmcacheFrozen.description: Indicates whether the Read RAM Cache is currently temporarily not in use.

RmcacheMemoryAllocationState.description: Indicates the state of the memory allocation process (in progress/done).

rfcacheErrorInconsistentCacheConfigurati.description: Indicates whether there is an error in the Read Flash Cache configuration.

rfcacheErrorInconsistentSourceConfigurat.description: Indicates whether there is an error on the Read Flash Cache source device.

authenticationError.description: Indicates the reason that the SDS failed to authenticate to the MDM.

rmPendingAllocatedInKb.description: Capacity that is marked for removal (for example, during a remove device/SDS operation).

semiProtectedVacInKb.description: The capacity of the other copy of the data stored on the SDS which is currently in maintenance mode. "Vac" represents allocated capacity.

inMaintenanceVacInKb.description: Capacity that is currently in maintenance (on the SDS that is currently in maintenance mode). "Vac" represents allocated capacity.

activeMovingInNormRebuildJobs.description: A counter that presents a backward rebuild that was triggered from the normal-need-rebuild state. The counter represents the number of combs that are currently being moved in the target SDS.

activeMovingOutNormRebuildJobs.description: A counter that presents a backward rebuild that was triggered from the normal-need rebuild state.

pendingMovingInNormRebuildJobs.description: Combs that should be rebuilt but are currently waiting in the queue.

primaryVacInKb.description: "primary" represents master MDMs in version 2.0 and later. "Vac" represents allocated capacity.

secondaryVacInKb.description: "secondary" represents secondary MDMs in version 2.0 and later. "Vac" represents allocated capacity.

SdsDelta.title: Sds-Delta
SdsDelta.description: An object that contains data regarding the changes that occurred in Sds objects in the cluster since the last request, and data regarding how to send the next query.

sessionTag.description: The session in which communication with the MDM regarding the delta is done. It is echoed back to the MDM in the next call within this session. Use 0 for the initial call.

isDirty.description: TRUE indicates that changes to the data queried occurred during the processing of the request. These changes may or may not be reflected in the response. Depending on the use case, sending an additional query to check for missed changes may be advisable.

lastSdsVersion.description: Identifier within the session to be used in the next call. Use 0 in the initial call.

sdsIsPartial.description: TRUE indicates that only partial data was sent due to the response size passing the threshold defined in MDM. The remaining data should be retrieved using another query with the sessionTag + lastSdsVersion values returned by this query.

sdsList.description: List of added/modified SDS objects since the last query. In case of a full response (deletedSdsIdList = null) this list contains all the SDSs that currently exist (unless sdsIsPartial=TRUE, in which case this list is a subset of all currently existing SDSs)

deletedSdsIdList.description:
List of IDs of deleted SDS objects since the last query. If the list is null (unlike an empty list), it means that the response contains all instances of SDS objects; any SDS objects not included in this response should be considered as deleted.
NOTE: This attribute may be set in conjunction with the sdsIsPartial attribute, in which case only SDS objects that are not present in this response and any of the subsequent responses until a non-partial response was received were actually deleted. If your use-case is sensitive to bursts of delete-and-re-create events, this should be taken into account.

volumeAddressSpaceInKb.description: The sum of user defined sizes for volumes and snapshots. So if you create a 1TB volume this will display 1TB regardless of if you performed any I/Os on the volume, and if you snapshot it once the counter will be 2TB.

netTrimmedUserDataCapacityInKb.description: The capacity that was trimmed or unmapped by user I/Os. For implementation reasons sometimes when you do that we can’t just delete the data, we have to save a metadata record that it was deleted. Overall this number doesn’t really matter, but our metadata size is limited to allow up to x10 compression, and these trims also takes these resources.

netProvisionedAddressesInKb.description: The logical size the data takes before taking compression into account. This is applicable only for base volume, not for the snapshots.

netUserDataCapacityNoTrimInKb.description: This is NetUserDataCapacityInKb minus NetTrimmedUserDataCapacityInKb. We generally display and use this counter, since trims are rarely important.

ActualNetCapacityInUseInKb.description: The physical size the data takes after taking compression into account. For a thick VTree it will show how much capacity it would have taken if it was thin instead. This is only really useful for VTree migration purposes.

NetThinUserDataCapacityInKb.description: The logical size the data takes before taking compression into account. This is applicable for thin capacity only.

TrimmedCapacityInKb.description: VTree counter, this is how many trims happened on the VTree, not actual capacity saved in the system. So if you trim 100GB on the VTree it will display 100GB, even if you repeatedly do it on the same address for instance. Not very useful in my opinion.

VTreeAddressSpaceInKB.description: The sum of user defined sizes for volumes. If you create a 1 TB volume this will display 1 TB regardless of if you performed any I/Os on the volume, and if you snapshot it once the counter will be 2TB.

ReplicationPair.title: ReplicationPair
ReplicationPair.description:
The ReplicationPair object corresponds to a replication pair of local and remote volumes.

**Note**: The properties that are added in the brackets are only availabe when you create a new ReplicationPair. These properties are not displayed when retrieving it.

localVolumeId.description: name of the property for POST: sourceVolumeId

remoteVolumeId.description: Required - name of the property for POST: destinationOVolumeId

errorState.description:
Indicates the reason that a device is in an error state:
None—the device is fine
Error—there is a problem with the device that is causing I/Os to fail
Warning
Notice
Info
Acceleration— the device is fine, but the acceleration device it relies on is in error, so the device is unusable
Unrecoverable— the device is a fine granularity device and a checksum error was detected on it, or it's a fine granularity device which is not of type SSD

isDirty.description: TRUE indicates that changes to the data queried occurred during the processing of the request. These changes may or may not be reflected in the response. Depending on the use case, sending an additional query to check for missed changes may be advisable.

lastSdrVersion.description: Identifier within the session to be used in the next call. Use 0 in the initial call.

sdrIsPartial.description: TRUE indicates that only partial data was sent due to the response size passing the threshold defined in MDM. The remaining data should be retrieved using another query with the sessionTag + lastSdrVersion values returned by this query.

sdrList.description: List of added/modified Sdr objects since the last query. In case of a full response (deletedSdrIdList = null) this list contains all the SDRs that currently exist (unless sdrIsPartial=TRUE, in which case this list is a subset of all currently existing SDRs)

deletedSdrIdList.description:
List of IDs of deleted Sdr objects since the last query. If the list is null (unlike an empty list), it means that the response contains all instances of Sdr objects; any Sdr objects not included in this response should be considered as deleted.
NOTE:	This attribute may be set in conjunction with the sdrIsPartial attribute, in which case only Sdr objects that are not present in this response and any of the subsequent responses until a non-partial response was received were actually deleted. SDC.title: SDC
SDC.description:
From Powerflex 4.0, an SDC object also represents NVMe host. This can be determined by examining the value of the hostType property. When an SDC object represents an NVMe host, all relationship URLs will return host URIs.
Host URIs are also available for getting all instance, specific instance, and statistic. Host URIs behaves same as the equivalent SDC URI. For example, instance will return a list of all hosts (both NVMe and SDC).

error-message.title: error-message
error-message.description: Message component in Error response (Power API spec)

severity.description:
The severity of the condition.
        * INFO - Information that may be of use in understanding the failure. It is not a problem to fix.
        * WARNING - A condition that isn't a failure, but may be unexpected or a contributing factor. It may be necessary to fix the condition to successfully retry the request.
        * ERROR - An actual failure condition through which the request could not continue.
        * CRITICAL - A failure with significant impact to the system. Normally failed commands roll back and are just ERROR, but may be used for exceptional cases.

arguments.description:
Ordered list of substitution args for the error message. Must match up with 
            the {0}, {1}, etc... actually in the message referenced by the message code 
            above, if any.
